confidence_rationale: Operational instantiation of FEP for the explore-exploit dilemma: agents minimise expected free energy, which decomposes cleanly into extrinsic value (utility) and epistemic value (information gain). The paper provides simulations showing this framing reproduces both directed exploration and softmax-style noise as Bayes-optimal precision parameters. Direct support for R1 both-channels-needed verdict from a computational theoretical anchor.
failure_signatures: If active inference's epistemic-value formulation is wrong, any MECH-314 instantiation that uses information-gain as the curiosity signal (vs novelty-bonus a la Wittmann 2008 or learning-progress a la Schmidhuber) loses its theoretical grounding.
If the precision-as-confidence framing is wrong, REE's existing precision-modulation substrates (MECH-269b, SD-032b) would also be affected since they share the same theoretical commitment.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Choice behaviour is modelled as minimisation of expected free energy of policies. Expected free energy decomposes into extrinsic value (expected utility / reward) and epistemic value (information gain about the causes of valuable outcomes). The explore-exploit dilemma is resolved by maximising epistemic value first, then maximising extrinsic value once information gain is exhausted. Softmax temperature parameters in standard RL emerge as Bayes-optimal precision of policy beliefs.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 R1 verdict: active inference provides a unified framework where structured exploration (epistemic value, MECH-314) and stochastic exploration (precision of policy beliefs, MECH-313) are not competing channels but complementary terms in a single objective. This supports the both-channels-needed reading from a computational angle. R4 verdict: epistemic value is continuous (computed every tick from current model uncertainty), but its dominance over extrinsic value is triggered (active when uncertainty is high; recedes as uncertainty falls). Hybrid continuous+triggered.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Active inference is a specific theoretical framework that REE has not formally committed to. The paper's simulations are toy environments (T-mazes); transfer to embodied open-ended grid-world REE is a non-trivial step. Mapping fidelity moderate.
mapping.source_context: Computational simulations + theoretical synthesis; UCL Wellcome Centre + Italian National Research Council collaboration; the operational active-inference paper that goes beyond the unified-theory framing of Friston 2010.

confidence_components.notes: Cognitive Neuroscience (lower-impact venue than NRN); operational paper with toy-environment simulations; mapping requires REE to commit (or not) to the active-inference framing for MECH-314 vs alternative pragmatic instantiations.
